---
description: Run clang-tidy static analysis on all C++ files modified since last commit. Groups findings by severity.
---

Run static analysis on all modified C++ files using `compile_commands.json` from the ESP-IDF build.

```bash
# Ensure compile_commands.json is up to date (generated by idf.py build)
if [ ! -f build/compile_commands.json ]; then
  echo "Run 'idf.py build' first to generate compile_commands.json"
  exit 1
fi

git diff --name-only HEAD | grep -E '\.(cpp|hpp|h|cxx|cc)$' | \
  xargs -r -I{} clang-tidy {} -p build/ --warnings-as-errors='*' 2>&1
```

Report findings grouped by severity: **error → warning → note**

For each finding include:
- File path and line number
- Rule name (e.g., `readability-identifier-naming`, `clang-diagnostic-unused-variable`)
- Description
- Suggested fix

Note: some ESP-IDF system headers may produce false positives — suppress them with
`// NOLINT(<rule>)` on the offending line, or add to `.clang-tidy` suppression list.
